key_insight: LATTICE is a graph self-supervised framework that fuses five aligned spatial omics modalities to learn spot-level tissue embeddings that better capture multimodal biology than RNA alone.
clinical_relevance: This approach may improve how spatial tumor samples are analyzed and stratified, but it is an analysis tool rather than a treatment and still needs broader validation before clinical use.
method: Builds a spatial neighborhood graph over Visium spots
Uses a TransformerConv encoder with masked reconstruction, cross-modal alignment, and spatial smoothness losses
Integrates Visium RNA, scMultiome RNA/ATAC, spatial ATAC, and spatial CUT&Tag into one latent embedding
evidence_signal: In a private 11-sample melanoma cohort with 54,912 total spots, adding scMultiome RNA to Visium RNA improved agreement with Space Ranger clusters across 11 runs (ARI +0.157, NMI +0.143, spatial contiguity +0.174), and additional modalities further improved spatial contiguity and MUS.
watch_item: The model may diverge from RNA-derived reference labels when chromatin and regulatory signals dominate, so it needs stronger supervision and external benchmarking on independent cohorts.
paper_title: LATTICE: Graph Self-Supervised Learning for Multimodal Spatial Omics Integration
